homepage.text_excerpt: Charlie Maynard MP Skip to main content Charlie Maynard MP Meet Charlie Contact Charlie Charlie's Campaigns Volunteer News Donate Join Donate Join Charlie's Campaigns Rebuilding Our Railway Clean Water and Fair Bills Backing Local Farmers Improving Local Healthcare Supporting Our Highstreets Cost of Living Crisis District Council Elections 2026 News Subscribe Charlie Maynard MP Charlie is Witney's MP - Fighting for a fair deal for our home Charlie covers the Witney Constituency, including the towns of Witney, Carterton, Faringdon, Burford & the surrounding villages. Meet Charlie Maynard MP Meet Charlie Maynard Charlie Maynard is the Liberal Democrat MP for Witney, elected in 2024 to represent the towns of Witney, Carterton, Faringdon, Burford and the surrounding villages. A long-time campaigner for better local transport and sustainable development, he’s committed to giving West Oxfordshire the voice it deserves. Charlie lives near Witney with his wife Sophie and their children. His family has welcomed refugees from Syria and Ukraine into their home, reflecting his belief in compassion, fairness, and community. In his previous role as a District Councillor, Charlie has campaigned on issues ranging from sewage pollution to the NHS and sensible planning and infrastructure development for a number of years. Even before entering politics, Charlie was campaigning to tackle the major traffic issues on the A40 by reinstating the Oxford to Carterton rail line. As MP, his campaigns also include fair water bills for residents, backing local farmers, improving the health care availability locally, backing our highstreets and supporting residents through the cost of living crisis. pages.title: Meet Charlie - Charlie Maynard MP
headings: h2: Member of Parliament for Witney constituency
h2: Sign up for email updates...
pages.text_excerpt: Meet Charlie - Charlie Maynard MP Skip to main content Charlie Maynard MP Meet Charlie Contact Charlie Charlie's Campaigns Volunteer News Donate Join Donate Join Charlie's Campaigns Rebuilding Our Railway Clean Water and Fair Bills Backing Local Farmers Improving Local Healthcare Supporting Our Highstreets Cost of Living Crisis District Council Elections 2026 News Subscribe Meet Charlie Maynard Member of Parliament for Witney constituency Charlie has a background in business, is centrist and practical in political outlook, and has been a local councillor on West Oxfordshire District Council since 2022. He lives with his family between Witney and Stanton Harcourt. Charlie ran to become Witney's MP because he wants to get useful things done for the local community, help the country’s economy and address our environmental problems. Charlie spent three years volunteering as Chair of the Witney Oxford Transport Group, leading the project to rebuild the railway line linking Carterton, Witney, Eynsham and Oxford. The aim is to reduce traffic on the A40 and have a long-term plan for our area which protects our beautiful countryside while also keeping it economically vibrant with jobs and sufficient housing, especially for young people. Charlie strongly believes that his first job as an MP is to help people who most need help. As well as volunteering for local charities and helping out at the local GP surgery, Charlie and his wife Sophie have hosted several Syrian and Ukrainian refugees in their home—helping them find jobs or establish their own businesses, go to university or reunite with family. Stopping climate change is the major challenge of our age. Charlie has a strong interest in renewable energy and commissioned a study from Renewables First on the hydropower potential of the weirs on the Upper Thames with a view to establishing a community hydro project, www.upperthameshydro.com , similar to what has already been achieved by communities at Sandford, Osney and Reading. With his family at the Devil's Quoits, Stanton Harcourt Charlie and Sophie celebrating with their Syrian friend Anas and his children on receiving their British citizenship With the family's much loved dog, the late Sisi Exploring hydropower options with David Jeffcoat around Shifford Weir Charlie's career has been in business. Aged 25, he co-founded BDA Partners , an Asia-focused advisory firm. He spent 24 years working with colleagues to build the business, which now employs more than 120 people across nine global offices. He decided to step back from the business in January 2021, part of a long-term plan to spend the second part of his life being useful . Charlie has overcome challenges, whether it's recovering from breaking his neck aged 18 or building a global business. He believes in hard work, in trusting people and working as a team to solve problems. He knows how important it is to keep going. He is much more focused on what unites us than what divides us. pages.title: Charlie's Campaigns - Charlie Maynard MP
headings: h2: Rebuilding Our Railway
h3: Charlie is fighting to rebuild a railway from Oxford to Witney and Carterton, and improve transport in West Oxfordshire.
h2: Clean Water and Fair Bills
h3: Taking on Thames Water, with the help of WASP, Charlie is fighting to keep sewage out of our rivers and to stop our bills from rising further.
h2: Backing Local Farmers
h3: Charlie knows how difficult life is for our farmers. They deserve better, and we need to back them to keep producing high-quality food and protecting our countryside.
h2: Improving Local Healthcare
h3: Working closely with local and regional NHS trusts, Charlie is working on improving secondary & social care availability in local communities.
h2: Supporting Our Highstreets

h3: Rising costs, staffing pressures and a lack of long-term government strategy are putting town centres under strain, but Charlie is working with business leaders and pushing in Parliament to make sure our shops and small enterprises can thrive.
h2: Cost of Living Crisis
h3: Charlie has been working hard in the constituency and at Westminster to tackle the cost of living crisis.
h2: Sign up for email updates...
